author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
kabe vṛṣabhānupure, āhirī gopera ghare,
tanayā hoiyā janamiba

jāvaṭe āmāra kabe, e pāṇi⋅grahaṇa habe,
vasati kariba kabe tāya
sakhīra parama śreṣṭha, je tāhāra haya preṣṭha,
sevana kariba tā̐ra pāya

te̐ha kṛpāvān haiñā, rātula caraṇe lañā,
āmāre karibe samarpaṇa
saphala hoibe daśā, pūribe manere āśā,
se du̐hāra jugala caraṇa

vṛndāvane duijana, caturdike sakhīgaṇa,
sevana kariba avaśeṣe
sakhīgaṇa cāribhite, nānā jantra laiñā hāte,
dekhiba manera abhilāṣe

du̐hu cā̐damukha dekhi’, juḍābe tāpita ā̐khi,
nayane bahibe aśrudhāra
vṛndāra nideśa pāba, dohāra nikaṭe jāba,
hena dina hoibe āmāra

śrī⋅rūpamañjarī sakhī, more anāthinī dekhi,
rākhibe rātula duṭī pāya
narottama⋅dāsa bhaṇe, priyanarmasakhīgaṇe,
kabe dāsī karibe āmāya
en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever come for me again? When will I be born as a daughter in the home of a cowherd in the village of Vṛṣabhānu?

When will my life be accepted in the village of Yāvaṭa, and when will I reside there? I will serve at the feet of the greatest of the companions, who is most dear to Rādhā.

She, becoming merciful and taking me to her reddish feet, will surrender me to the Divine Couple. Then my life will be successful, and my heart's desires will be satisfied at Their twin feet.

In Vṛndāvana, I will finally serve the Divine Couple, who are surrounded by companions on all sides. I will see the companions all around, holding various instruments in their hands, fulfilling the desire of my heart.

Seeing Their moon-like faces, my burning eyes will be cooled, and streams of tears will flow. I will receive the order of Vṛndā and go near the Two. Will such a day come for me?

Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, seeing me as a helpless orphan, will keep me at her two reddish feet. Narottama dāsa says: when will she make me a maidservant among the dear intimate companions?
